task.title: Mirroring a network interface by using nmcli

li.p: A network interface to mirror the network traffic to.

context.p: Network administrators can use port mirroring to replicate inbound and outbound network traffic being communicated from one network device to another. Mirroring traffic of an interface can be helpful in the following situations:

li.p: To debug networking issues and tune the network flow
To inspect and analyze the network traffic

step.cmd: Add a network connection profile that you want to mirror the network traffic from:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type ethernet ifname enp1s0 con-name enp1s0 autoconnect no

step.cmd: Attach a qdisc of type prio to enp1s0 for the egress (outgoing) traffic with the 10: handle:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify enp1s0 +tc.qdisc "root prio handle 10:"
info.p: The qdisc set to prio attached without children allows attaching filters.

step.cmd: Add a qdisc for the ingress traffic, with the ffff: handle:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify enp1s0 +tc.qdisc "ingress handle ffff:"

step.cmd: Add the following filters to match packets on the ingress and egress qdiscs, and to mirror them to enp7s0:

info.codeblock:
# nmcli connection modify enp1s0 +tc.tfilter "parent ffff: matchall action mirred egress mirror dev enp7s0"
# nmcli connection modify enp1s0 +tc.tfilter "parent 10: matchall action mirred egress mirror dev enp7s0"
info.p: The matchall filter matches all packets, and the mirred action redirects packets to destination.

li.p: Display the traffic mirrored on the target device (enp7s0):

p.xref: How to capture network packets using tcpdump (Red Hat Knowledgebase)
